# Harmonia

> Unified self-hosted media platform: acquisition, organization, serving, playback, requests, integrations, and desktop/mobile surfaces.

Harmonia is a Rust workspace meant to replace the arr stack with one coherent media lifecycle manager. The backend owns storage, import, metadata, downloads, HTTP/OpenSubsonic serving, QUIC renderer transport, and audio playback.

## Binary

`harmonia` - main CLI/server binary from `crates/archon`, with modes for serving, database/migration work, playback, rendering, and migration.

## Architecture

Structured LLM corpus:
- [_llm/architecture.toml](_llm/architecture.toml): workspace layers, crate roles, and interfaces
- [_llm/current_state.toml](_llm/current_state.toml): phase, recent work, and open threads
- [_llm/decisions.toml](_llm/decisions.toml): accepted media-platform decisions
- [_llm/glossary.toml](_llm/glossary.toml): harmonia vocabulary
- [_llm/apis.toml](_llm/apis.toml): CLI and HTTP surfaces

## Agent Entry Points

- [README.md](README.md): overview and capability status
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): repo conventions
- [docs/architecture/](docs/architecture/): subsystem and crate architecture
- [docs/VISION.md](docs/VISION.md): product vision
- [standards/](standards/): kanon-synced standards
